step_by_step_thinking:
Electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) can have various short-term side effects, but anterograde amnesia is one of its more frequent complications. This refers to difficulty forming new memories after treatment sessions begin. While retrograde amnesia may occur as well due to seizure activity affecting brain regions involved with memory consolidation during sleep cycles, it tends not to be as prevalent or severe compared to anterograde amnesia post-therapy initiation.

Therefore, I select option A: Antegrade Amnesia.
Your analysis is thorough and accurate. Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT) commonly leads to temporary cognitive changes such as anterograde amnesia because these treatments affect recent memory formation processes. The other potential complications like psychosis or depression are less frequently reported immediate adverse events following an ECT session when compared to issues related to memory loss.
